SENTENCE: Hello, my name is Frank Duffy,
Co-Chair of Innovation Reporting Team (IRT).
Hello, my name is Raymond Merritt, Jr.,
the other Co-Chair.
The IRT was established by the President to collect,
organize, prioritize and report innovative ideas
for transforming Gallaudet University.
We need your help to complete this task.
It's exciting that Gallaudet has established the IRT
to collect your innovative ideas.
We seek these ideas to promote and support
our new Gallaudet Strategic Plan, GSP.
Innovation is important for achieving the five goals
of the GSP aimed to increase student enrollment,
increase graduation rates, expand funding,
optimize career success for students,
and to advance in research and development.
We need you to share your ideas between now and June.
There are two key words
that we need to pay attention to:
transformation and innovation.
Here's a way to think about those words.
Imagine a butterfly.
A butterfly is not a caterpillar with wings attached.
The caterpillar must transform
to become the butterfly.
It must change significantly.
That's what transformation is - significant changes
that create new ways of thinking, new ways of working,
and new ways of helping the university
achieve its strategic goals.
Those "new ways" are your innovative ideas.
Proposing small changes to programs and departments
is not innovation.
Proposing to keep everything we have
but with small adjustments is not innovation.
Innovation requires big ideas
that will transform the university.
So, please, think big, think new, and think innovative.
Gallaudet needs change.
There is no limit to innovation except our imagination.
We will be open to all innovative ideas,
whether it is big or small, or whether it is in the interest
of the University, department, or individual.
